_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the find-skills skill: does a real `/charness:find-skills` run honor its own central claims, above all that it routes to its references at the point of need? declaredReferences is the full reference set (coverage report). referenceEngagement classifies each reference on the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the locked methodology contract (charness-artifacts/spec/2026-06-23-skill-claim-fidelity-doc-philosophy.md). requiredCommandFragments are the engage-always references SKILL.md routes to in a representative run: if the run never opens one, it did not follow its own routing. thresholds are intentionally omitted until a first baseline capture sets a per-skill runtime budget. requiredCommandFragments rationale: These two docs define the ordering of the search (discovery-order) and the classification labels applied to each match (support-consumption), forming the core decision frame for any representative capability-discovery run.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: Specifies how the harness can be widened via adapter configuration; consulted only when trusted-skill-roots or other optional adapter fields are present.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: when the host has configured trusted skill roots or other adapter extensions beyond the default local-first discovery

discovery-order.md.rationale: Defines the canonical search ordering that any capability discovery run executes (local public > support > synced support > integrations > trusted roots > missing).

session-start-routing.md.rationale: Explains the routing decision when a session starts and the skill must invoke the next workflow; a pure 'which skill handles X?' query treats the inventory answer as the deliverable without this doc.
session-start-routing.md.trigger: when a session opens with a pickup (bare handoff mention) or named workflow to execute, requiring the skill to drive the routed workflow

support-consumption.md.rationale: Provides the classification vocabulary and rationale for step 4 of the workflow (classify the best match honestly), distinguishing public skill, support skill, trusted skill, synced support skill, external integration, and missing capability layers.
